name: Hot Pumpkin Praline Souffle W/Creme Anglaise
recipeInstructions:
1. Place milk and vanilla in a heavy pot and bring to a boil.
2. In a mixing bowl, place yolks and sugar; beat with an electric mixer until pale yellow (approximately 10 minutes).
3. Add flour, and pour half of the milk over the egg mixture and beat.
4. Pour the egg mixture back into the remaining milk and return to the stove. Heat until thick, stirring constantly.
5. Strain and chill.
6. When cold, stir in pumpkin puree and praline paste.
7. To make soufflé:
a. Butter and sugar individual ramekins.
b. In a bowl, place 1 cup of soufflé base mixture.
c. In the mixer bowl, beat egg whites until thick and creamy.
d. With a rubber spatula, fold egg whites into the base mixture.
e. Pour into prepared ramekins and bake in an oven at 375 degrees for approximately 10 minutes (until puffed and set).
f. Remove from oven and sprinkle tops with powdered sugar; serve.
g. Break a hole in the center of the soufflé and spoon creme anglaise into the souffle.
CREME ANGLAISE
Place egg yolks and sugar in the bowl of an electric mixer and beat on high speed until the mixture is pale and forms ribbons (approximately 10 minutes).
In a separate heavy-bottom pot, place half-and-half, salt, and split vanilla bean. Bring cream to a boil and remove from the stove.
Pour half of the boiled cream over the egg mixture while beating on low speed.
Pour the egg mixture back into the cream and return to the stove.
On low heat, stirring constantly with a wooden spoon, heat until the mixture lightly thickens (the mixture should coat the back of a wooden spoon; be careful not to let it get like an egg scrambled).
Remove from Stove.
Strain and chill.
